accepted_findings: The 20.23 m effective-width asset loads at publisher scale, retains its explicit material, and renders through the UE 5.8 Metal Nanite path.
The new command produces paired guide-seat and river-eye evidence from the unchanged source map and adds only transient, non-colliding review actors.
Eight bounded 0.94-1.58x instances can be evaluated without saving a substituted corridor map or changing Landscape, water, collision, solver, or gameplay authority.
rejection_reasons: The fragments read as small pasted-on rocks against hundred-metre smooth gorge walls; they do not replace or convincingly break up the 30 m DSM-derived canyon silhouette.

Several faces collapse to near-black under the fixed corridor photometry, leaving abrupt high-contrast marks instead of integrated weathered cliff structure; an isolated orientation, normal, Nanite-usage, and backface audit is required before reuse.
Only 1.92 percent of guide-seat pixels and 0.94 percent of river-eye pixels change, so this placement strategy cannot materially close the current lifelike terrain gap.
The repeated transforms are camera-relative review probes rather than source-aligned outcrops, joint sets, talus, bedding, or mapped Batoka landforms.
Namaqualand geology is not Batoka basalt authority, and no geologist, guide, hazard-readability, desktop, or VR performance approval exists.
next_iteration_requirements: Keep the asset under ExternalReview and excluded from default Zambezi map generation.
Run a neutral isolated turntable that distinguishes mesh orientation, backface, normal-map, Nanite material-usage, and lighting failures before any further scene placement.
Acquire or author several source-matched jointed-basalt wall, column, ledge, overhang, and talus modules with measured metre scale and non-repeating silhouettes.
Bind any future cliff modules to geospatially reviewed wall masks, slope/aspect, river station, and terrain contact rather than camera-relative offsets.
Prefer the confirmed Batoka LiDAR/photogrammetry acquisition path for gorge-scale geometry, then rerun guide, geology, art, hazard, rights, and packaged desktop/VR review.
